message.content:
Attack this consolidated plan. Default refuted=true if uncertain.

THESIS: The contract is not merely too strict — it is INVERTED, and I proved it end-to-end through production code: `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/cellcog_composer.py:403` fires the gate ONLY on sentences that do NOT match `[Ww]riting in the`, so ATTRIBUTED sentences (name a source; where fabrication is fraud) are 100% UNGATED while OWNED sentences (the reviewer's voice; explicitly permitted to be non-entailed) are the only ones checked — by rules that reject 97% of the prose the judge scored 9.8/10. The exact fabricated binding the CI canary exists to catch is REJECTED in the canary's phrasing and ADMITTED when phrased in the form `WRITE_PROMPT:296` MANDATES; all 4 canary checks pass and it prints "GATE IS WIRED AND ON THE CRITICAL PATH." Fixing the inversion is simultaneously the safety fix, the biggest score lever on the board (the INSIGHT dimension, w=0.32, is entirely non-entailed relations — the class we delete — worth +0.0463, 39% of the gap), and the generality fix (VERDICT_VOCAB is 17 hardcoded English idioms and SAFE_CAPS hardcodes `AI/Artificial/Intelligence/Fourth/Industrial/Revolution` — task-72 overfit sitting inside the safety gate, rejecting 100% of Chinese prose across 50 of the 100 tasks).
WHEEL: FIX -> COMPOSE -> CHEAP TEST (zero judge calls) -> SCORE AT CRITERION LEVEL -> READ THE JUDGE'S PROSE -> FIX. All scripts exist; none is new.

1. FIX + COMPOSE: `python scripts/cellcog_composer.py --write` in /home/polaris/wt/flywheel.

2. CHEAP TEST FIRST — THIS IS THE GATE ON SPENDING MONEY. Run `scripts/test_gate_is_wired.py` (rewritten, Turn 1) plus the turn's own mechanism counter. K5 is non-negotiable: A SCORE RUN IS INADMISSIBLE AS EVIDENCE ABOUT A LEVER WHOSE POST-CLEANER COUNTER DID NOT MOVE. If the counter is flat, do not spend the $0.11. This repo's history is levers that moved a metric while the mechanism never fired (commits 225b323, 947d2b5) — the counter is the only thing that stops it recurring.

3. SCORE AT THE CRITERION LEVEL: `python scripts/criterion_ab.py --a outputs/rank10_sections_compose/report.md --b <new>/report.md --task-id 72 --k 5 --targets "<the EXACT criteria this turn names>"`. This is the whole methodological correction: 20 of 25 criteria cannot clear a scalar kill rule EVEN AT 10/10, so a lever judged on the scalar is judged on an instrument that cannot see it. Judge the lever on the criterion it targets; carry the other 24 as a regression guard.

4. READ THE ORACLE'S PROSE: `outputs/judge_feedback.json` carries the judge's WRITTEN per-criterion analysis for BOTH articles. The harness computes it and throws it away (`deepresearch_bench_race.py:158`). It told us "extremely long paragraphs... a fragmented narrative" and named the exact eleven consultancies it can see. It answers for $0.11 what these plans budget days to argue about. Read it every turn, before theorising.

5. THE SCALAR IS A REPORT, NOT A STEER. Two new facts force this. (a) The reference is NOT constant: R = 8.033/7.545/7.363 vs us/bodhi/cellcog. (b) NEWLY MEASURED BY ME, AND NOBODY MODELLED IT: the drag is NEGATIVE on 23 of 25 criteria but +2.0 on the journal criterion — the judge marks the REFERENCE UP on the very criterion our best "free" lever targets. So that lever is worth +0.0070, not the +0.0093 the plan books, and it CANNOT clear the +0.0094 resolvable effect standalone. It is not a bad lever; it is an UNMEASURABLE-IN-ISOLATION lever. Stack it, and score it at the criterion (1.5 -> 9.0 is not noise).
TURNS: [{"n":1,"name":"THE VOICE ROUTER + THE CANARY THAT ACTUALLY BINDS (the keystone; buys zero score; nothing else is possible without it)","change":"Build `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/voice.py`: `classify(sent, ledger) -> ATTRIBUTED | OWNED | CENSUS`. Rewrite `cellcog_composer.py:381-411 _clean()` into `_route(md, premises)`. ATTRIBUTED -> binding + number-provenance + mechanism-binding + span-entailment (reject on NEUTRAL *and* CONTRADICTED). OWNED -> no source named, no digit, no new entity, reject ONLY on CONTRADICTED; NON-ENTAILMENT EXPLICITLY PERMITTED. KILL THE `_SYNTH_CARDS` MODULE GLOBAL (`:324`, written `:434-435`, read `:403`) — 6 threads (`max_workers=6`, `:441`) write and read one global, so the gate today validates subsection A's prose against subsection B's premises. Pass premises as an argument. REWRITE THE CANARY FIRST, BEFORE ONE GATE IS RELAXED: `test_gate_is_wired.py:47-51` asserts only that `validate` appears in an `ast.Call` — satisfied by calling it on the wrong lane — and its own check #4 calls `validate()` in isolation, the exact closed-loop failure it was written to prevent. The new canary must drive the real `_route()` entry point.","targets_criteria":["(none — this turn is INFRASTRUCTURE and I price it at zero. Its targets are two ERROR RATES, not a criterion. It unblocks turns 2/3/4, and it stops us shipping fabrications today.)"],"expected_score_units":"+0.000. I will not claim a point for it. It is the highest-priority turn anyway, for two reasons that have nothing to do with score: (1) we are shipping fabrications RIGHT NOW — the ATTRIBUTED lane is 100% ungated and the prompt mandates the exact phrasing that bypasses the gate; (2) turns 3 and 4 are literally impossible until the router exists, and they are where the score is.","cheap_test":"Zero judge calls, already written and run — /tmp/prove_inverted_gate.py drives the real `_clean()`. TODAY: the fabricated binding in the canary's phrasing -> REJECTED; THE SAME FABRICATION in the phrasing WRITE_PROMPT:296 MANDATES ('Writing in the Quarterly Journal of Economics in 2002, Bresnahan and colleagues show that ... is driven by task displacement') -> ADMITTED TO THE PAGE. AFTER: both REJECTED. Second counter, also measured: false-rejection on cellcog's Section 5 (79 sentences, scored 9.8/10) must fall from 97% (77/79 — reproduced: 41 die to VERDICT_VOCAB, 33 to any-digit, 3 to FORECAST) to <=15%. My prototype router already hits 9%.","kill":"If ATTRIBUTED-lane span-entailment rejects >25% of attributed sentences, the SPANS are the defect, not the prose — the extraction gate only verifies the first 60 chars (`cellcog_composer.py:162`), leaving tails unverified. STOP and fix the extractor. DO NOT RELAX THE GATE TO MAKE THE NUMBER LOOK GOOD. That is the move that created this bug."},{"n":2,"name":"THE ATTRIBUTED LANE FIRES — fix the input, then open the channel","change":"`cellcog_composer.py:345-354 _fmt_cards()` hands the writer `FINDING: {c['claim']}` (`:350`) — the LLM's own paraphrase, prompted 'in your words' (`:118`) — and NEVER shows the span. The verified quote is an admission ticket, then discarded. A gate whose ground truth is the claim CERTIFIES fabrication rather than catching it. Hand the writer THE SPAN. Then open the channel: every citable claim carries author+year+journal INSIDE the sentence (the only form that survives RACE's `clean_prompt.py`, which deletes reference lists, [n] markers and footnotes from every submission before the judge reads a word). Delete the 345 [n] markers. We currently ship 345 markers and 0 in-prose journal attributions; the judge sees 3 journal names and 11 consultancy names and names exactly those eleven in its 1.5/10 complaint. We are graded on a corpus we did not submit.","targets_criteria":["Exclusive Citation of 'High-Quality Journal Articles'","Depth and Representativeness of Literature Synthesized","Exclusive Citation of 'English-Language' Journal Articles","P1: Clarity and Synthesis in Presenting Sourced Information"],"expected_score_units":"+0.008 to +0.015 (ceiling for the channel trio, measured with the per-criterion reference response: +0.0178). NOTE THE HONEST CORRECTION I MEASURED AND NOBODY ELSE DID: the journal criterion alone is +0.0070, NOT the +0.0093 the plan books, because the judge marks the REFERENCE UP +2.0 (2.0 -> 4.0) on that same criterion as the target improves. It therefore CANNOT clear the +0.0094 resolvable effect standalone. Stack it; score it at the criterion (1.5 -> 9.0).","cheap_test":"Run RACE's own `prompt/clean_prompt.py` over the report and COUNT surviving (author, year, venue) triples. Today: ~10. Target: >=100. Zero judge calls. This is the K5 mechanism counter for this turn.","kill":"Post-cleaner surviving triples < 60 -> the channel did not open, the run is INADMISSIBLE as evidence (K5), and no judge call is spent. Separately: if the coverage matrix is NOT fillable from journals alone, SCOPE THE DECLARATION TO WHAT IS TRUE. A declaration we cannot honor is precisely the fabrication we are trying to kill."},{"n":3,"name":"THE OWNED LANE FIRES — this is the band jump, and we have been gating it shut","change":"DELETE from the OWNED lane, in `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/synthesis_contract.py`: VERDICT_VOCAB (`:71-76`) + rule 8 (`:232-234`) — 17 hardcoded English idioms that alone reject 41/79 of cellcog's 9.8/10 prose and 100% of Chinese; FORECAST (`:84`) + rule 7a (`:227-228`) — bans 'will', making 'Value and Foresight' (w=0.048) LITERALLY UNWRITABLE; UNIVERSAL (`:87`) + rule 7b (`:229-230`) — bans 'none', making COVERAGE_GAP unpassable; rule 10 anchoring (`:247-249`) — punishes abstraction, the thing insight pays for; the task-72 tokens in SAFE_CAPS (`:96-111`: AI, Artificial, Intelligence, Fourth, Industrial, Revolution, Firm, Worker, Task, Aggregate, Economy). KEEP rule 4 (no digit — numbers belong to ATTRIBUTED) and rule 5 (no new entity). Then WRITE: >=8 named, counted, first-person tagged syntheses. cellcog scores 
INTEGRITY: THE CONTRACT, REWRITTEN AROUND VOICE. Fabrication = an ATTRIBUTED sentence its source does not entail. Insight = an OWNED sentence its premises do not entail. SAME LOGICAL SHAPE; distinguished by WHOSE VOICE, not by entailment. That is why no entailment threshold can separate them, and why the current gate — which rejects on NEUTRAL — deletes 97% of the prose scored 9.8/10 while leaving the fraud lane wide open.

WHAT SHIPS (all deterministic except where marked LLM):
- `voice.py`: `classify(sent, ledger) -> ATTRIBUTED | OWNED | CENSUS`. A sentence may not be both. THIS IS THE MOAT: YOU CANNOT LAUNDER AN INFERENCE THROUGH AN ATTRIBUTION.
- ATTRIBUTED lane (names a source; carries the numbers, years, N's; fabrication banned absolutely): (a) BINDING — the named source is in this paragraph's premise set; (b) NUMBER PROVENANCE — every digit-string appears in THAT card's SPAN, not its claim [this check does not exist today and it is the one that makes numeric fabrication impossible]; (c) MECHANISM BINDING — every asserted mechanism is in THAT card's span-gated `mechanisms` [the existing FABRICATED_BINDING check at synthesis_contract.py:214-224 is CORRECT and is wired to the WRONG LANE]; (d) SPAN ENTAILMENT (LLM) — reject on NEUTRAL *and* CONTRADICTED. NEUTRAL-rejection is right HERE and only here.
- OWNED lane (reviewer's voice; may not name a source): no digit; no entity absent from the premise blob; NON-CONTRADICTED by premises (LLM; reject ONLY on CONTRADICTED); NON-ENTAILMENT EXPLICITLY PERMITTED, BECAUSE THAT IS WHAT INSIGHT IS.
- CENSUS lane: every number rendered from `len()` of a ledger collection. The LLM never emits it, so it cannot fabricate it.
- `_fmt_cards` hands the writer THE SPAN, not the LLM's paraphrase (`cellcog_composer.py:350` ships `claim`, prompted 'in your words').

WHAT IS DELETED: VERDICT_VOCAB + rule 8; FORECAST + rule 7a; UNIVERSAL + rule 7b; rule 10 (anchoring); the task-72 tokens in SAFE_CAPS; the `_SYNTH_CARDS` module global (a live 6-thre
EXPECTED: NO. Honestly: the stack lands at ~0.478-0.521 against a 0.4382 baseline. It probably does not beat bodhi (0.5420) and it does not beat cellcog (0.5603). Here is the arithmetic, computed from the judge's own per-criterion scores with the per-criterion reference response applied (which nobody had done, and which makes the picture slightly WORSE, not better):

  BASELINE (pinned k=5)                                     0.4382   (n=1 recompute: 0.4413)
  + ATTRIBUTED lane / channel      ceiling +0.0178  ->  book +0.008..0.015
  + OWNED lane / insight           ceiling +0.0463  ->  book +0.015..0.030
  + CENSUS (marginal, shares CS)                     ->  book +0.005..0.012
  + SHAPE (7 readability crit)     ceiling +0.0259  ->  book +0.012..0.026
  ------------------------------------------------------------------------
  REALISTIC                                              0.478 .. 0.521
  EVERYTHING THE JUDGE NAMED, MAXED TO CELLCOG'S OWN SCORES = 0.5019   (55% of the gap; FINAL_PLAN said 0.5071 — it is 0.5019 once the journal criterion's +2.0 reference anchoring is included)

WHAT IS MISSING, NAMED EXACTLY, AND IT IS NOT IN MY ANGLE: COMPREHENSIVENESS. +0.0246 sits in 'Scope of Industry-Specific Analysis' (w=.0725, 6.8 -> 9.3), 'Breadth of Labor Market Restructuring Dimensions' (w=.0725, 8.5 -> 9.8), 'Grounding in 4IR' (6.5 -> 9.4), 'Exploration of Disruptive Character' (8.0 -> 9.2). THAT IS A CORPUS PROBLEM, NOT A COMPOSITION PROBLEM. Only 17 of 120 journal works are on-topic; the corpus contains ResNet, the BMJ PRISMA checklist, and a 1974 paper on reading automaticity. NO FAITHFULNESS ARCHITECTURE REPAIRS A CORPUS THAT IS AIMED WRONG. SELECT is the broken stage (OPERATOR_FIX 2), and the coverage matrix + evidence-TYPE diversity axis (FINAL_PLAN 1.3) is what owns it. If that is not fixed in parallel, THAT is what makes us miss, and I am naming it now rather than after.

WHERE I DIFFER FROM FINAL_PLAN, AND IT IS THE ONE THING THAT COULD CLOSE THE GAP: FINAL_PLAN books the insight residual as an unmechanized band jump — 'not levers... the document having a thesis... a hypothesis, not a priced lever, and it is the thing that will make us miss.' THAT IS WRONG. All five insight criteria (w=0.32 — 'Analytical Depth in Characterizing Mechanisms', 'Emergent Themes/Theoretical Linkages', 'Critical Synthesis', '4IR Integration', 'Value and Foresight') ARE NON-ENTAILED RELATIONS — the exact class the OWNED lane admits and our own contract deletes (97%, measured, and FORECAST makes 'Value and Foresight' literally unwritable). The band jump HAS a mechanism. It is the OWNED lane. FINAL_PLAN proposed the contract fix and never connected it to the residual it despaired of. Priced at cellcog's own scores: +0.0463 — 39% of the entire gap from one architectural change.

AND THE ONE PLACE WE CAN STRUCTURALLY BEAT THE #1 SYSTEM: cellcog's WEAKEST criterion of 25 is 'Exclusive Citation of High-Quality Journal Articles' — 8.0/10, w=0.0375, headroom 2.0. It scores 8.0 because it is a WRITER that SAYS it uses journals and MOSTLY does. WE ARE A SYSTEM: we can enforce journal-only on the CITABLE pool DETERMINISTICALLY, in code, and make the declaration TRUE BY CONSTRUCTION. All 25 at cellcog's level + that one criterion at 9.8 = 0.5621 > 0.5603. That is the ONLY path I can find that beats it, the margin is +0.0018 — BELOW the judge SD of 0.0074 — and it requires matching the #1 system on twenty-four other criteria first. I am not going to dress that up as a plan. P(beat 0.5420) ~ 30%. P(beat 0.5603) ~ 10-15%.

ATTACK ON ALL OF:
1. **ARITHMETIC**: do the turns, priced in SCORE units, actually sum to >0.5603? Remember R DROPS as we improve
   (proven), so every lever is worth ~20% more than an R-fixed model says. Check the sum honestly.
2. **DEAD LEVERS**: does it resurrect anything that DIED? (30+ subsections = well-powered null. Length beyond 8k =
   saturated. "Document shape is worthless" = a units error, but so was "+5.0 is the biggest lever".)
3. **FABRICATION**: under the ATTRIBUTED/OWNED contract, trace a concrete path by which a false claim ships.
4. **THE UNMEASURED**: does it actually MEASURE generality, or just assert it? 38 runs, all task 72.
5. **WASTE**: is any turn unmeasurable at the criterion level? A lever we cannot resolve is a lever we cannot keep.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
the sentence is the only thing that survives.)

2. FACTS -- you may ONLY state findings from the evidence above. Every number must come from a card.
   Do not add a fact, a number, a study, or an organisation that is not in the evidence.

3. ADJUDICATE -- do not merely list. Where findings agree, say what they jointly establish. Where they
   conflict, say WHY they can both be true (different unit of analysis? horizon? method?) and state what
   the evidence does NOT settle. Use verdict language: "establishes", "does not establish", "is limited
   to", "cannot distinguish", "remains unresolved".
   CRITICAL: you may NOT invent a causal explanation. If no source states a mechanism, you may say the
   findings differ in level/horizon/method, but you may NOT say WHY the world behaves that way.

4. LABEL YOUR JUDGEMENTS -- where you reach a conclusion, tag it inline exactly like this:
     **[Established finding]** / **[Contested]** / **[Unresolved]**
   Use these sparingly (at most one per paragraph) and only where the evidence supports the label.

5. OPEN EACH PARAGRAPH WITH A CLAIM, not a topic announcement. No "This section discusses...".
   Never mention this pipeline, retrieval, or "the question above".

Return ONLY the markdown prose for this subsection (no heading -- I add it). No preamble."""


# ----------------------------------------------------------------- step 2: compose

_SYNTH_CARDS: list = []   # set per-subsection; the premises the gate validates against

BANNED_META = re.compile(
    r'\b(this report|this review synthesi[sz]es|the pipeline|retrieved|the question above|'
    r'span-grounded|telemetry|our system|we retrieved|the corpus)\b', re.I)
MARKER = re.compile(r'\[\d+\]')
PAREN_YEAR = re.compile(r'\((?:19|20)\d\d[a-z]?\)')


def _select(cards, sub, k=10):
    """Pick the cards most relevant to this subsection (lexical overlap on content words)."""
    want = {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', sub.lower())}
    scored = []
    for c in cards:
        blob = f"{c['claim']} {c['level']} {c['method']} {' '.join(c['mechanisms'])}".lower()
        have = {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', blob)}
        scored.append((len(want & have), c))
    scored.sort(key=lambda x: -x[0])
    return [c for s, c in scored[:k] if s > 0] or [c for _, c in scored[:4]]


def _fmt_cards(sel):
    out = []
    for c in sel:
        mech = f" | mechanism stated by the paper: {', '.join(c['mechanisms'])}" if c['mechanisms'] else ''
        out.append(
            f"- FINDING: {c['claim']}\n"
            f"  ATTRIBUTION (use this exact wording): {c['attribution']}\n"
            f"  unit of analysis: {c['level'] or '?'} | horizon: {c['horizon'] or '?'} | "
            f"method: {c['method'] or '?'}{mech}")
    return '\n'.join(out)


def _gate_synthesis(sent: str, cards_in_para: list[dict]) -> tuple[bool, str]:
    """THE GATE, ON THE CRITICAL PATH. This is the call that was missing.

    `validate()` was imported at :49 and never invoked anywhere in the repo except its own self_test().
    The gate was a closed loop -- fed its own hand-written examples, printing green, never seeing a
    sentence from the pipeline. Behind it, 43% of our evidence-card mechanisms were pure invention.
    A test that passes because the gate returns True in isolation is worth nothing.
    """
    prem = {}
    for i, c in enumerate(cards_in_para):
        prem[f'p{i}'] = Premise(
            id=f'p{i}', text=c['claim'], source=c['source'],
            level=c.get('level', ''), horizon=c.get('horizon', ''),
            method=c.get('method', ''), mechanisms=c.get('mechanisms') or [])
    if len(prem) < 2:
        return False, 'fewer_than_2_premises'
    for op in OPERATIONS:
        ok, _ = validate(Synthesis(op, list(prem), sent), prem)
        if ok:
            return True, op
    _, why = validate(Synthesis('CONTRASTS_LEVEL', list(prem), sent), prem)
    return False, why


def _clean(md: str) -> tuple[str, list[str]]:
    """Deterministic post-gate. Drops any sentence that breaks the contract. Never repairs."""
    dropped = []
    keep = []
    for para in md.split('\n\n'):
        p = para.strip()
        if not p:
            continue
        if p.startswith('#'):          # the model was told not to, but never trust it
            continue
        sents = re.split(r'(?<=[.!?])\s+', p)
        good = []
        for s in sents:
            if MARKER.search(s):
                dropped.append(f'citation-marker: {s[:60]}')
                continue
            if BANNED_META.search(s):
                dropped.append(f'meta-commentary: {s[:60]}')
                continue
            # A sentence that names no source is a SYNTHESIS claim. It must pass the contract.
            # (This is the call that never existed. Without it the only thing between LLM prose and the
            #  page was this regex.)
            if _SYNTH_CARDS and not re.search(r'[Ww]riting in the|,\s+in the\s+\*', s) and len(s.split()) > 8:
                ok, why = _gate_synthesis(s, _SYNTH_CARDS)
                if not ok:
                    dropped.append(f'GATE[{why}]: {s[:60]}')
                    continue
            good.append(PAREN_YEAR.sub('', s))    # a parenthetical year is deleted by the cleaner anyway
        if good:
            keep.append(' '.join(good).strip())
    return '\n\n'.join(keep), dropped


def write_report() -> int:
    cards = json.loads(CARDS.read_text())
    print(f'=== composing from {len(cards)} span-verified evidence cards '
          f'({len({c["doi"] for c in cards})} journal articles) ===\n')
    OUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    jobs = [(sec, sub) for sec, subs in OUTLINE for sub in subs]

    def one(job):
        sec, sub = job
        sel = _select(cards, sub)
        if not sel:
            return job, '', []
        p = WRITE_PROMPT.format(section=sec, sub=sub, cards=_fmt_cards(sel))
        try:
            raw = llm(p, max_tokens=3000)
        except Exception as e:
            print(f'  ! {sub[:40]}: {e}')
            return job, '', []
        raw = re.sub(r'^```(?:markdown)?|```$', '', raw.strip(), flags=re.M).strip()
        global _SYNTH_CARDS
        _SYNTH_CARDS = sel          # the gate validates against THIS subsection's cards
        body, dropped = _clean(raw)
        return job, body, dropped

    results = {}
    all_dropped = []
    with futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=6) as ex:
        for job, body, dropped in ex.map(one, jobs):
            results[job] = body
            all_dropped += dropped
            w = len(body.split())
            print(f'  [{w:>4}w] {job[1][:62]}')

    # assemble
    md = ['# The Restructuring Impact of Artificial Intelligence on the Labor Market',
          '', '## Abstract', '',
          '**Objective.** This review examines how artificial intelligence, as the defining '
          'general-purpose technology of the Fourth Industrial Revolution, is restructuring the labor '
          'market across industries.', '',
          '**Methods.** The review draws exclusively on peer-reviewed, English-language journal '
          'articles identified through citation-graph expansion from the canonical literature on '
          'automation and work.', '',

          'productivity gains in controlled settings, while aggregate employment effects remain '
          'contested and are not established at the level of the economy.', '',
          '**Contributions.** The review distinguishes exposure from adoption and adoption from '

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""THE SYNTHESIS CONTRACT — the "deep thinking" half, without selling the faithfulness moat.

THE PROBLEM, PRECISELY
----------------------
POLARIS's entailment gate (entailment_judge.py:588) kills any sentence that introduces
"a fact, entity, MECHANISM ... NOT present in the SPAN". A cross-source inference IS, by definition,
a mechanism present in neither span. So our own gate deletes exactly the sentence type that earns
INSIGHT — 0.32 weight, the heaviest dimension, and our worst score (0.4238 vs bodhi's 0.5457).

We are not bad at analysis. We built a machine that cannot do it.

THE INSIGHT THAT MAKES THIS SOLVABLE
------------------------------------
Look at what the winning systems' synthesis prose actually asserts. cellcog, verbatim:

    "The three frameworks are complementary rather than competitive. SBTC remains the clearest model
     for relative skill demand. The task-based framework subsumes the phenomena both describe within
     a more general model of endogenous task allocation."

NO new fact. NO number. NO new entity. It RANKS and RELATES claims already on the page.
That is ADJUDICATION, and it is fully compatible with a no-fabrication guarantee.

So we do not relax the gate. We add a SECOND lane with a DIFFERENT PROOF OBLIGATION:

    EVIDENCE sentence  -> must be span-grounded in ONE source (the existing gate, UNCHANGED)
    SYNTHESIS sentence -> must be a TYPED RELATION over premises that are THEMSELVES already admitted

A synthesis sentence may not introduce a fact; it may only assert a relationship between facts that
are already proven. Its proof obligation is structural, not evidential.

THE HARD RULE THAT KEEPS US HONEST
----------------------------------
    An explanatory MECHANISM may appear ONLY if a premise span states that mechanism.
    Otherwise POLARIS may identify a contrast but MAY NOT explain its cause.

So this is legal:
    "Firm-level expansion and worker-level displacement are not contradictory: the two estimates
     observe different units of analysis."          [CONTRAST_LEVEL over two admitted premises]
And this is NOT, unless 'adoption lag' is stated in a premise:
    "The difference probably reflects slower regional adoption."   [imports an unproven mechanism]

Run the adversarial suite:  python scripts/synthesis_contract.py --self-test
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import re
import sys
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from pathlib import Path

# ---------------------------------------------------------------- the typed operations

OPERATIONS = {
    'CONVERGES':            'two or more premises agree on direction/finding',
    'CONTRASTS_DIRECTION':  'premises disagree on the direction of an effect',
    'CONTRASTS_LEVEL':      'premises differ because they observe different units (task/worker/firm/region/economy)',
    'CONTRASTS_HORIZON':    'premises differ because they observe different time horizons',
    'CONTRASTS_METHOD':     'premises differ because they use different identification strategies',
    'BOUNDARY_CONDITION':   'a premise holds only within a stated scope',
    'RANK_EVIDENCE':        'one premise rests on a stronger design than another (declared fields only)',
    'ESTABLISHES':          'the admitted evidence supports a claim AT A STATED LEVEL',
    'DOES_NOT_ESTABLISH':   'the admitted evidence does NOT support a claim at a stated level',
    'REMAINS_UNRESOLVED':   'the admitted evidence cannot distinguish between the competing accounts',
    'COVERAGE_GAP':         'a cell of the evidence matrix is empty — a real, derivable research gap',
}

# The rubric rewards ADJUDICATION, not hedging. This is the verdict vocabulary.
VERDICT_VOCAB = (
    'establishes', 'does not establish', 'supports', 'is limited to', 'cannot distinguish',
    'remains unresolved', 'are not contradictory', 'is consistent with', 'rests on',
    'observe different', 'holds only', 'no evidence', 'the evidence cannot', 'differ because',
    'complementary rather than', 'subsumes', 'rather than',
)

# A synthesis sentence may NEVER assert a causal mechanism of its own.
CAUSAL_IMPORT = re.compile(
    r'\b(because of|due to|caused by|driven by|reflects|reflecting|owing to|as a result of|'
    r'attributable to|explains why|the reason is|stems from|arises from|leads to|results in)\b', re.I)

# Forecasts / predictions are always fabrication in a literature review.
FORECAST = re.compile(r'\b(will|shall|is going to|by 20[3-9]\d|in the coming|future will|predict|forecast)\b', re.I)

# Universal quantifiers = overclaim.
UNIVERSAL = re.compile(r'\b(all|every|always|never|none|no one|invariably|universally|certainly|proves)\b', re.I)

NUMERIC = re.compile(r'\d')
SPELLED_QTY = re.compile(
    r'\b(doubl|tripl|quadrupl|halv|tenfold|twofold|threefold|a third|a quarter|a half|two[- ]thirds|'
    r'percent|percentage|majority|most of|vast majority)\w*\b', re.I)
CAP_TOKEN = re.compile(r"\b([A-Z][A-Za-z&.\-']{1,})\b")

# Discourse vocabulary that may be capitalised without being a new entity.
SAFE_CAPS = {
    'The', 'This', 'These', 'Those', 'Taken', 'Together', 'Both', 'Neither', 'Either', 'While',
    'Whereas', 'Although', 'Because', 'When', 'Where', 'If', 'In', 'At', 'By', 'For', 'A', 'An',
    'It', 'They', 'Their', 'Its', 'However', 'Yet', 'Still', 'Across', 'Within', 'One', 'Two',
    'Three', 'Four', 'Five', 'Six', 'Seven', 'Eight', 'Nine', 'Ten', 'AI', 'Artificial',
    'Intelligence', 'Fourth', 'Industrial', 'Revolution', 'Evidence', 'Studies', 'Research',
    'Together', 'Read', 'Seen', 'Firm', 'Worker', 'Task', 'Aggregate', 'Nor', 'That', 'What',
    # ANALYTIC META-VOCABULARY: words that describe the EVIDENCE, not entities in the world.
    # These are the declared-field names ('level', 'horizon', 'method') and the language of
    # adjudication. Admitting them cannot fabricate anything — a fabricated NAME would still be
    # caught, because it would not appear in any premise.
    'level', 'Level', 'unit', 'Unit', 'analysis', 'Analysis', 'scope', 'Scope', 'design', 'Design',
    'horizon', 'Horizon', 'method', 'Method', 'estimate', 'Estimate', 'estimates', 'Estimates',
    'outcome', 'Outcome', 'outcomes', 'Outcomes', 'finding', 'Finding', 'findings', 'Findings',
    'economy', 'Economy', 'result', 'Result', 'results', 'Results', 'literature', 'Literature',
    # THE EPISTEMIC TAXONOMY. cellcog is the ONLY system on the board that labels its own insight and
    # the judge scored it 9.8/10 for exactly that, naming all eight tagged syntheses. The gate was
    # rejecting '[Unresolved]' as a FABRICATED ENTITY. These are discourse markers about the STATUS OF
    # A CLAIM — they assert nothing about the world and cannot fabricate anything.
    'Established', 'Contested', 'Unresolved', 'Emerging', 'Conceptual', 'Analytical', 'Hypothesis',
    'Pattern', 'Synthesis', 'Our', 'We', 'Proposed', 'Argument',
}


@dataclass
class Premise:
    """An ALREADY-ADMITTED evidence sentence — it passed the existing span-grounding gate."""
    id: str
    text: str
    source: str                      # attribution string, e.g. "Autor et al. (2003), QJE"
    level: str = ''                  # task | worker | firm | occupation | region | economy
    horizon: str = ''                # short-run | long-run
    method: str = ''                 # experiment | quasi-experimental | observational | survey
    mechanisms: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)   # mechanisms STATED IN THE SPAN


@dataclass
class Synthesis:
    operation: str
    premise_ids: list[str]
    text: str


def _stem(w: str) -> str:
    """Crude stemmer. Needed because 'task level' must anchor to a premise that says 'tasks',

        tok = re.sub(r"'s$", '', tok)     # strip the possessive: "AI's" -> "AI"

    # 6. NO IMPORTED MECHANISM, AND NO FABRICATED BINDING.
    #    A fabrication can be assembled ENTIRELY FROM TRUE PARTICULARS: bind a REAL mechanism to a REAL
    #    paper that never states it. Found live on disk: "task displacement" (Autor-Levy-Murnane's term)
    #    attributed to Bresnahan et al. (2002), whose span never says it. No "new entity" rule catches
    #    that -- both the mechanism and the paper are real. THE LIE IS IN THE BINDING.
    #    So we check TWO things: (a) is a causal mechanism asserted at all, and (b) if the sentence NAMES
    #    a source, does THAT source's own span state the mechanism it is being credited with?
    m = CAUSAL_IMPORT.search(s)
    if m:
        stated = {mm.lower() for p in prem for mm in p.mechanisms}
        if not stated:
            return False, f'causal_language_with_no_stated_mechanism:"{m.group(0)}"'
        if not any(mech in s.lower() for mech in stated):
            return False, f'causal_language_names_a_mechanism_no_premise_states:"{m.group(0)}"'
    # (b) THE BINDING CHECK -- runs whether or not causal vocabulary is present.
    #     If the sentence names an author from a premise AND asserts a mechanism, that mechanism must be
    #     stated by THAT author's premise -- not by some other paper in the room.
    for p in prem:
        surname = p.source.split()[0].rstrip(',')
        if len(surname) < 4 or surname.lower() not in s.lower():
            continue                      # this source is not named in the sentence
        for other in prem:
            if other is p:
                continue
            for mech in other.mechanisms:
                if mech.lower() in s.lower() and mech.lower() not in {x.lower() for x in p.mechanisms}:
                    return False, (f'FABRICATED_BINDING: "{mech}" is stated by {other.source.split()[0]}, '
                                   f'not by {surname} — the sentence credits the wrong paper')

    # 7. NO FORECAST, NO UNIVERSAL
    if FORECAST.search(s):
        return False, f'forecast_or_prediction:"{FORECAST.search(s).group(0)}"'
    if UNIVERSAL.search(s):
        return False, f'universal_overclaim:"{UNIVERSAL.search(s).group(0)}"'

    # 8. (REMOVED AS A HARD GATE.) This demanded "verdict vocabulary" of every uncited sentence and
    #    DELETED 163 sentences in wheel turn 1 — gutting the report to 4,021 words, below the length
    #    floor. It is a STYLE RULE WEARING A SAFETY RULE'S CLOTHES: a sentence that reads as a "vibe"
    #    is weak prose, not a fabrication. Adjudication is what the RUBRIC pays for; the CONTRACT's job
    #    is only to make lying impossible. Conflating the two is why our gate rejects 97% of the prose
    #    that the judge scores 9.8/10. Style belongs in the writer's PROMPT, not in the safety gate.

    # 9. operation-specific structural obligations, checked against DECLARED fields only
    if syn.operation == 'CONTRASTS_LEVEL':

            return False, 'CONTRASTS_LEVEL but the premises do not declare 2 distinct levels'

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""CI CANARY — FAILS IF THE FAITHFULNESS GATE IS BYPASSED.

WHY THIS EXISTS, IN ONE PARAGRAPH:
I built `synthesis_contract.py`, ran 14 adversarial attacks I had written myself, watched it print
"ZERO FALSE ADMISSIONS", and reported it as working. An adversarial reviewer then found that
`validate()` was imported at `cellcog_composer.py:49` and **never called anywhere in the repo except
its own self_test()**. The gate was a closed loop: invoked only by its own test, fed its own examples,
printing green. It had never seen a sentence from the pipeline. Behind that unlocked door, 43% of our
evidence-card mechanisms were pure LLM invention.

A self-test that passes because the gate returns True in isolation is worth NOTHING.
This test FAILS IF THE GATE IS NOT ON THE CRITICAL PATH. That is a different thing, and it is the only
kind of test that would have caught the bug.

    python scripts/test_gate_is_wired.py
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import ast
import json
import re
import sys
from pathlib import Path

ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
COMPOSER = ROOT / 'scripts' / 'cellcog_composer.py'
CARDS = ROOT / 'outputs' / 'evidence_cards.json'

fails: list[str] = []


def check(name: str, ok: bool, detail: str = '') -> None:
    print(f"  [{'PASS' if ok else '**FAIL**'}] {name}")
    if detail:
        print(f"            {detail}")
    if not ok:
        fails.append(name)


print('=== CI CANARY: IS THE FAITHFULNESS GATE ACTUALLY ON THE CRITICAL PATH? ===\n')

src = COMPOSER.read_text()
tree = ast.parse(src)

# 1. validate() must be CALLED, not merely imported. This is the exact bug that shipped.
calls = {n.func.id for n in ast.walk(tree)
         if isinstance(n, ast.Call) and isinstance(n.func, ast.Name)}
check('validate() is CALLED in the composer (not just imported)',
      'validate' in calls,
      'imported at :49 and never called — the gate has never seen a real sentence' if 'validate' not in calls else '')

# 2. the mechanism field must be span-gated at extraction
mech_gated = bool(re.search(r'mechanism gate|m_words\s*&\s*span_words', src))
check('the `mechanisms` field is span-checked at extraction',
      mech_gated,
      'mechanisms copied raw from LLM output -> 43% pure invention' if not mech_gated else '')

# 3. no card on disk may carry a mechanism absent from its own span
if CARDS.exists():
    cards = json.loads(CARDS.read_text())
    norm = lambda s: re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', (s or '').lower())
    bad = []
    for c in cards:
        sw = {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', norm(c.get('span')))}
        for m in (c.get('mechanisms') or []):
            mw = {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', m.lower())}
            if mw and len(mw & sw) / len(mw) < 0.6:
                bad.append((m, (c.get('authors') or ['?'])[0], c.get('year')))
    check(f'zero fabricated mechanisms in {CARDS.name}',

content.text:
Verified against the live worktree at `/home/polaris/wt/flywheel`.

**What survives:** the inversion itself. `scripts/cellcog_composer.py:403` reads
`if _SYNTH_CARDS and not re.search(r'[Ww]riting in the|,\s+in the\s+\*', s) and len(s.split()) > 8:` — the gate fires only on sentences that do *not* name a source. Attributed sentences are ungated; `WRITE_PROMPT` mandates the bypassing phrasing. `_SYNTH_CARDS` (`:324`, written `:434`, read `:403`) is a module global under `max_workers=6` (`:441`). Both real. Turn 1 is correct and should ship.

**What refutes the plan:** three load-bearing measurements are computed against a file state that no longer exists, and the "generality fix" is a safety inversion of its own.

input.reasoning:
TURN 1 SURVIVES; THE PLAN DOES NOT. I confirmed the inversion in production code: cellcog_composer.py:403 gates only sentences that do NOT match `[Ww]riting in the`, so the ATTRIBUTED lane is 100% ungated while WRITE_PROMPT mandates that exact phrasing; and `_SYNTH_CARDS` is a module global (:324/:434/:403) read under `max_workers=6` (:441), so subsection A's prose is validated against subsection B's premises. Ship the router, kill the global, rewrite the canary. But the plan's SCORE case and its GENERALITY case both fail on the live code.

(B) THE BIGGEST LEVER IS BOOKED AGAINST DEAD CODE. Turn 3 (+0.0463, "39% of the gap") opens by deleting "VERDICT_VOCAB (:71-76) + rule 8 (:232-234) — 17 hardcoded English idioms that alone reject 41/79 of cellcog's 9.8/10 prose." Rule 8 IS ALREADY GONE. `git show HEAD:scripts/synthesis_contract.py | grep -n VERDICT_VOCAB` returns exactly ONE line: 71, the tuple definition. It is referenced nowhere. Commit 038615b removed the hard gate; the file's own comment at :249 says so, and quotes the same 97% figure the plan quotes as a live defect. The plan is citing the repo's own post-mortem as if it were a bug. 41 of the claimed 77 false rejections (53 of the 97 points) come from a gate that CANNOT FIRE. Deleting it moves no counter — and by the plan's OWN K5 rule, a lever whose post-cleaner counter cannot move is inadmissible as evidence. The live rules can produce at most 33 (any-digit) + 3 (FORECAST) = 36/79 = 46%, not 97% — and the plan explicitly KEEPS rule 4 (any-digit) in the OWNED lane. So the deletions relieve 3 sentences plus rule 10. The prototype's "9% false rejection" is therefore produced by RE-ROUTING the 33 digit sentences to ATTRIBUTED, not by the deletions — and those 33 then face span number-provenance, which the plan's own kill rule concedes may reject >25%. The +0.015..0.030 booking has no mechanism behind it.

(C) THE GENERALITY CLAIM IS BACKWARDS, AND THE FIX IS A FAIL-OPEN. Every English artifact in the contract is a REJECT TRIGGER, so on Chinese it MATCHES NOTHING AND ADMITS. CAP_TOKEN is `\b([A-Z]...)\b` — it cannot match a Chinese character, so rule 5 (new entity) is a no-op on Chinese. CAUSAL_IMPORT, FORECAST, UNIVERSAL: no-ops. VERDICT_VOCAB: dead. I ran validate() on Chinese premises with a Chinese synthesis that invents a government agency, an imported mechanism and a universal forecast ("差异源于国务院统计局的数据滞后，未来所有岗位都将被取代。"). It is rejected — by ONE rule: `not_anchored_in_its_premises`. That is RULE 10, the rule the plan proposes to DELETE ("rule 10 anchoring (:247-249) — punishes abstraction"). Delete it and, on the 50 Chinese tasks, the OWNED lane's entire deterministic moat evaporates: invented agency, invented mechanism, invented forecast, all ADMITTED, with only a CONTRADICTED-only LLM check between them and the page. The plan converts a fail-CLOSED no-op into a fail-OPEN no-op on half the benchmark and calls it "the generality fix." It also never measures it: every scoring command in the plan is `--task-id 72`.

(D) CONCRETE FABRICATION PATH UNDER THE NEW CONTRACT (English). OWNED keeps only rule 4 (no digit) and rule 5 (no new entity), rejects only on CONTRADICTED, and the plan moves mechanism-binding to ATTRIBUTED — so rule 6 (CAUSAL_IMPORT / imported mechanism, synthesis_contract.py:214-224) guards nothing in OWNED. Ship this: "The aggregate null and the firm-level expansion are not contradictory: displaced workers are reabsorbed through new task creation." No digit, no new entity, no source named, NEUTRAL not CONTRADICTED. It is an invented causal mechanism in the reviewer's voice, and no reader or judge can see the lane tag. The moat slogan ("you cannot launder an inference through an attribution") is true and irrelevant — the plan opens the REVERSE channel: launder a fabrication through the reviewer's voice. And the dilemma is unpriced: keep rule 6 in OWNED and you keep the single rule that most suppresses "Analytical Depth in Characterizing Mechanisms," which is where the +0.0463 was supposed to come from.

(E) ARITHMETIC. The plan itself lands 0.478-0.521 vs cellcog 0.5603 and states EXPECTED: NO. Turn 1 is self-priced at +0.000. Turn 3's +0.015..0.030 dies to (B). Turn 2's journal criterion is +0.0070, below the +0.0094 resolvable effect — the plan says so. What remains cannot clear 0.5420. The one named path to beat 0.5603 is +0.0018, below the judge SD of 0.0074, i.e. unmeasurable by construction (attack axis 5: a lever we cannot resolve is a lever we cannot keep).
input.fix:
1. SHIP TURN 1 ONLY, AS SAFETY, AT ZERO SCORE — as the plan itself prices it. Route on voice; gate the ATTRIBUTED lane (binding + number-provenance-against-the-SPAN + mechanism-binding); pass `premises` as an argument and DELETE the `_SYNTH_CARDS` module global; rewrite `test_gate_is_wired.py` to drive the real `_route()` entry point rather than asserting `validate` appears in an `ast.Call`. This is real and it is free.

2. BEFORE BOOKING ONE POINT OF TURN 3: re-derive the false-rejection counter against HEAD, not against the pre-038615b file. VERDICT_VOCAB and rule 8 are already dead (`git show HEAD:scripts/synthesis_contract.py | grep -n VERDICT_VOCAB` -> line 71 only, unreferenced). Delete the tuple as hygiene, claim nothing for it. Then measure what the LIVE rules actually reject on cellcog's Section 5 and price the OWNED lane off THAT number. My reading says the live relief is ~3 FORECAST sentences plus rule 10; the rest is the router re-classifying digit sentences, which is turn 1, already priced at zero.

3. DO NOT DELETE RULE 10. It is the ONLY rule in the contract that fires on non-Latin text — I proved it: a Chinese sentence inventing an agency, a mechanism and a universal forecast is rejected today by `not_anchored_in_its_premises` and by nothing else. If it "punishes abstraction," replace it with a script-agnostic anchor (character-ngram / CJK-aware token overlap), do not remove it. Port rules 5/6/7 to script-agnostic implementations, and add a Chinese adversarial case to the self-test, BEFORE any turn is allowed to use the word "generality." The generality claim is currently asserted on zero non-72 measurements and my one measurement contradicts it.

4. KEEP A MECHANISM RULE IN THE OWNED LANE, or the fabrication simply changes lanes. The cellcog form the plan admires already solves this: an OWNED causal assertion must either name the premise that states the mechanism, or be tagged **[Hypothesis]** / **[Unresolved]** inline. That preserves the insight class (a tagged conjecture is still a non-entailed relation and still scores) without shipping an untagged invented mechanism in the reviewer's voice. Price the OWNED lane only after this constraint is in it — the +0.0463 was measured on prose that does not carry it.

5. Turn 2 (hand the writer the SPAN, not `c['claim']` at :350; open the in-prose author+year+venue channel that survives `clean_prompt.py`) is the one lever here with a mechanism, a counter that can move, and no safety cost. It is also worth +0.008..0.015 and does not close the gap. Run it, score it at the criterion, and stop pretending the stack beats 0.5603 — the plan's own arithmetic already says it does not.
